evals.prompt: We just decided to use Postgres for the job queue instead of Redis — we benchmarked both and Postgres handles our volume fine and removes a dependency. Log this decision.
evals.expected_output: The agent greps decisions/ for existing redis or job-queue notes, finds none, creates decisions/YYYY-MM-DD-postgres-for-job-queue.md with full YAML frontmatter (type: decision, tags including type/decision and status/active, decision_date, status: active), writes a body with Context/Decided/Why/Open sections, updates the relevant MOC to link the new note, and confirms briefly with the filename.
expectations: The agent greps decisions/ before creating any file to check for an existing node on this topic
The created file is placed in decisions/ with a YYYY-MM-DD- prefix in the filename (e.g. decisions/2026-07-22-postgres-for-job-queue.md)
The frontmatter includes type: decision, status: active, decision_date, and tags containing type/decision
The note body uses the Context/Decided/Why/Open structure, not free-form prose
The agent updates a relevant MOC to link the new decision note and confirms with the exact filename

evals.prompt: I have a decision note at decisions/2024-11-03-use-redis-for-job-queue.md that we're now reversing — we're moving to Postgres. How should I handle the old decision note, and what do I need to update?
evals.expected_output: The agent explains the supersession pattern: set status: superseded on the old note, create the new decision note with supersedes: pointing to the old note's wikilink, and update any MOC entries. It does not recommend deleting the old note.
expectations: The agent instructs setting status: superseded on the old note (decisions/2024-11-03-use-redis-for-job-queue.md), not deleting it
The agent specifies adding a supersedes: field to the new decision note containing the full-path wikilink [[decisions/2024-11-03-use-redis-for-job-queue.md]]
The agent mentions bumping updated: on the old note when its status field is changed
The agent instructs updating the relevant MOC to point to the new decision, not the superseded one
The agent avoids recommending deletion of the old decision note

evals.prompt: I want to add a note about my new collaborator Dana Chen — she's the ML infra lead at a partner org we're working with on the data pipeline project. What should the note look like and where does it go?
evals.expected_output: The agent describes creating people/dana-chen.md with YAML frontmatter (type: person, title, aliases, tags including type/person and role/collaborator, created, updated, related linking to the relevant org and topic notes), a brief prose body, and updating the people MOC. It uses full-path wikilinks for all cross-references.
expectations: The agent places the note at people/dana-chen.md (kebab-case filename in the people/ folder)
The frontmatter includes type: person and tags containing type/person and role/collaborator
The related field uses full-path wikilinks (e.g. [[orgs/partner-org]], [[topics/data-pipeline]]) not bare [[dana-chen]] or markdown links
The agent specifies updating the people MOC (or a domain MOC) to include [[people/dana-chen|Dana Chen]] with a one-line context description
The agent avoids recommending markdown-style links ([Dana](./people/dana-chen.md)) for inter-wiki references
